big_picture: The government cut India’s FY27 GDP growth forecast to 6% citing risks from the West Asia war, while the Reserve Bank of India’s Monetary Policy Committee maintained the repo rate at 5.25% in a neutral stance. The Finance Ministry launched a ₹2.5 lakh crore credit guarantee scheme to support businesses affected by the West Asia war. The Election Commission reported that 91 lakh names were deleted from West Bengal’s electoral rolls after judicial scrutiny under the Special Intensive Revision (SIR) process. The Appellate Tribunal for SIR in West Bengal restored the Indian National Congress candidate’s name to the voter list after the Election Commission could not furnish reasons for deletion. The National Human Rights Commission issued a notice to the Health Ministry, FSSAI, and Delhi Police over alleged action taken against whistleblowers who raised concerns. Parliament passed the Andhra Pradesh Reorganisation (Amendment) Act, 2026, declaring Amaravati as the capital of Andhra Pradesh. Civil society groups raised concerns about growing censorship during a consultative meeting with the IT Ministry on proposed changes to the IT Rules, 2021.

story_clusters.headline: Moody's cuts India's FY27 GDP growth forecast to 6% amid West Asia war
story_clusters.summary: Moody's has reduced India's FY27 GDP growth forecast to 6% due to ongoing West Asia tensions. Gold prices surged as oil prices declined following a de-escalation in the US-Iran conflict, while fiscal risks are expected to rise amid the regional instability. The government is reportedly preparing a multi-pronged strategy to mitigate the economic impact of the West Asia war.

story_clusters.headline: Rs 2.5 Lakh Cr Credit Guarantee Scheme For Businesses Affected By West Asia War : Govt
story_clusters.summary: The Indian government is implementing a Rs 2.5 lakh crore credit guarantee scheme to support businesses affected by the West Asia crisis, offering up to 90% guarantees on loans up to Rs 100 crore. The Securities and Exchange Board of India (Sebi) has extended the validity of IPO and rights issue approvals until September 30, 2026, due to the ongoing geopolitical tensions. Additionally, the Confederation of Indian Industry (CII) has sought fiscal support from the Finance Ministry amid the West Asia crisis.

All three sources highlight rising cost pressures as a contributing factor.

Both parliamentary leaders acted on the same notices opposing the CEC's alleged role in the 2024 Lok Sabha elections.

perspectives.unique_angle: Assembly elections 2026 live: Campaigning ends in Kerala, Assam, Puducherry ahead of April 9 voting Assembly elections 2026 Live: Campaigning ended in Kerala, Assam and Puducherry, with voting set on

story_clusters.summary: Assembly elections in Kerala, Assam, and Puducherry concluded campaigning on April 8 ahead of voting on April 9. The Hindu National and HT India report that polling will take place on April 9 across the three regions.

story_clusters.headline: WhatsApp tests out SIM-binding norms for limited users

perspectives.title: WhatsApp tests out SIM-binding norms for limited users
perspectives.unique_angle: WhatsApp tests out SIM-binding norms for limited users On April 6, users flagged an in-app notification requesting SIM card verification

perspectives.title: WhatsApp confirms SIM binding testing for regular Indian users
perspectives.unique_angle: WhatsApp confirms SIM binding testing for regular Indian users Confirming the February WABetaInfo report that revealed WhatsApp is developing technical features needed to implement DoT's SIM binding d

story_clusters.summary: WhatsApp is testing SIM-binding norms for some regular users in India, as confirmed by the company. The feature aims to link user accounts to their phone numbers, according to reports from Hindu BusinessLine Tech and MediaNama. The testing is part of efforts to enhance account security and compliance with local regulations.

Civil Liberties.summary: The Parliament passed the Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Amendment Bill, 2026, which removes the provision allowing transgender individuals to self-identify their gender. The NHRC issued a notice to the Health Ministry, FSSAI, and Delhi Police after a complaint alleged retaliatory action against whistleblowers exposing alleged wrongdoing. Internal documents reveal evictions, potential violations of the Forest Rights Act, procedural lapses, and delays in India’s Green Credit Programme.
Civil Liberties.top_article_title: The Transgender Persons Amendment Bill, 2026, and its Discontents

Governance.summary: The Competition Commission of India directed 17 Opposite Parties to cease and desist from anti-competitive practices related to tenders for internal and external electrification works in police station buildings across Assam. CropLife India applauded the Plastic Waste Management Amendment Rules, 2026 for exempting the pesticide industry from mandatory recycled plastic use to maintain safety and regulatory alignment with CIB RC standards. The Indian Metals and Mining Association urged Maharashtra to retain its inspector-free fertilizer policy, opposing a proposed reintroduction of an inspector-driven regulatory system.
Governance.top_article_title: CCI directs 17 Opposite Parties to cease and desist from anti-competitive practices in relation to tenders invited by Assam Police Housing Corporation for “Internal and External Electrification works in Police Station Buildings” across the State

Legal.summary: The Supreme Court declined to interfere with the NCLAT order permitting Adani to acquire Jaypee Group’s assets and directed the appellate tribunal to expedite the petition filed by Vedanta Limited. The Delhi High Court ruled that the Lokpal must provide reasons for ordering a probe despite a CBI clean chit, thereby quashing the investigation against a DRI officer. The Calcutta High Court quashed an NDPS case against a petitioner, citing a 14-year delay and reliance solely on inadmissible Section 67 statements under the NDPS Act.
Legal.top_article_title: SC refuses to interfere with NCLAT order allowing Adani to acquire Jaypee group’s assets

Finance.summary:
The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) maintained the repo rate at 5.25% and kept its stance 'neutral'.

The Reserve Bank of India's Policy Meeting for April 2026 also left the repo rate unchanged at 5.25% with a 'neutral' stance.

Governor Sanjay Malhotra announced the Reserve Bank of India's decision to keep the repo rate at 5.25% and maintain a 'neutral' stance during the April 2026 Policy Meeting.
Finance.top_article_title: RBI MPC keeps repo rate unchanged at 5.25%, maintains 'neutral' stance

Politics.summary: The Lok Sabha passed the CAPF Bill with a voice vote as Opposition members protested and walked out. Om Birla will inaugurate the Commonwealth Parliamentary Association India Region West Zone conference in Goa on April 9 to discuss governance and development priorities. Shashi Panja questioned the fairness of the Bengal election, asking why so many genuine voters were left out due to adjudication.
Politics.top_article_title: Lok Sabha passes CAPF Bill with voice vote; Opposition members protest, walk out

Foreign.summary: India will host Bangladesh's Foreign Minister as External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar chairs a meeting to expand India-Africa ties in trade, investment, skilling, and defence. The US and Iran agreed to a provisional ceasefire, with Iran stating it will reopen the Strait of Hormuz. The US and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire brokered by Pakistan, with further talks invited to Islamabad.
Foreign.top_article_title: India steps up Africa outreach on defence, trade; to host Bangladesh's Foreign Minister

Defence.summary: The British Military is recruiting more Young Britons facing unemployment, addressing both a youth jobs crisis and military shortages. Iran's Supreme National Security Council has accepted a two-week ceasefire in the war, pending Trump's withdrawal of threats to target civilian infrastructure and reopen the Strait of Hormuz. The Indian embassy in Iran has advised its nationals to stay indoors for 48 hours and avoid military sites.
Defence.top_article_title: The British Military Sees Youth Jobs Crisis as an Opportunity

Trade.summary:
India remains largely unaffected by a 100% tariff on patented drugs after U.S. President Trump's executive order due to existing domestic production and policy shields.

The DGFT shifted wood pellet exports from prohibited to restricted and restricted wood briquette exports despite their earlier free status under a policy amendment.

The government notified Panoli Port in Gujarat as a customs area for import-export handling under the Customs Act, 1962 to expand logistics and strengthen regional trade infrastructure.
Trade.top_article_title: Patented drug tariffs: India more or less shielded, says GTRI

Energy.summary: The government introduced new rules for coal gasification mining plans to increase output. India confirmed it is continuing energy imports from Iran despite claims of cargo diversion over payment issues. An immigration expert stated that Indians are now considered as valuable as oil, coal, or gas in the context of H-1B visa debates.
Energy.top_article_title: Govt sets new rules for coal gasification mining plans to boost output

Technology.summary:
Civil society groups raised concerns about increasing censorship during an IT Ministry consultative meeting on proposed changes to the IT Rules, 2021.

MeitY Secretary stated that draft IT rules amendments are not connected to recent content takedowns.

The government imposed a December 31 deadline for apps to comply with SIM-binding requirements to strengthen security and user verification.
Technology.top_article_title: Civil society groups complain of growing censorship to IT Ministry

Urban.summary: The Centre notified the Delhi Development Authority’s new Transit-Oriented Development policy, prioritizing affordable housing in urban planning. Pune’s civic body reviewed urban planning failures after a sudden 15-minute rain caused severe waterlogging and traffic chaos, with experts attributing the crisis to poor drainage and unusual weather. The Centre approved the ‘as is, where is’ regularisation of 1,511 unauthorised colonies in Delhi, expected to boost real estate value, infrastructure, liquidity, and credit access.
Urban.top_article_title: Affordable housing at heart of DDA’s new TOD policy notified by Centre

Environment.summary: India’s updated climate pledges under its Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs) to the Paris Agreement highlight the tension between meeting climate commitments and addressing developmental costs. April Fonti’s The Story Keepers: Voices From A Changing Zanskar explores how climate change and social upheaval are impacting the remote Ladakh district of Zanskar. The standoff between nations over the next IPCC reports underscores the significance of disagreements in shaping the upcoming assessment cycle.

Telecom.summary:
TRAI released the Draft Telecom Consumer Protection (Thirteenth Amendment) Regulation, 2026 to introduce new consumer protection measures.

TRAI issued a Consultation Paper proposing a regulatory framework for Application-based Linear Television distribution (ALTD) Services, including Free Ad-Supported Streaming Television (FAST) Services.

The Business Standard report highlights that reforms under the SASCI framework are being linked to improving the right of way for telecom towers.
Telecom.top_article_title: TRAI releases Draft Telecom Consumer Protection (Thirteenth Amendment) Regulation, 2026

Welfare.summary: The BJP’s pre-poll outreach in Kerala includes promises of 'micro-minority status' and 'equitable access to welfare schemes, scholarships' for the Christian community. The BJP’s welfare schemes remain a key factor in Assam, outweighing voter unhappiness over the delayed ‘justice’ for singer Zubeen. In Upper Assam, the BJP’s welfare schemes give it an edge despite an upbeat Opposition campaign led by Gaurav, Akhil, and Lurunjyoti Gogois.
Welfare.top_article_title: How Christians are responding to BJP’s pre-poll outreach in Kerala

Education.summary:
A Class 4 student returned home drunk after consuming alcohol found in 23 liquor cartons seized from a Bihar government school toilet.

Tribal children in former Bastar Maoist strongholds are set to attend school for the first time in nearly two decades due to schools either closing or being demolished because of Naxal threats.

Odisha increased reservation quotas for Scheduled Castes, Scheduled Tribes, and introduced Other Backward Classes reservation in medical, technical education, universities, affiliated colleges, Industrial Training Institutes, and polytechnics.
Education.top_article_title: Class 4 student comes home drunk; 23 liquor cartons seized from Bihar govt. school toilet

Infrastructure.summary:
IRFC sanctioned a ₹1,000 crore term loan to Maharashtra State Power Generation Company (MAHAGENCO), its first deal of FY27, to support power capacity expansion in Maharashtra.

The National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) aims to reduce its debt to below ₹1.5 trillion in FY27 and become debt-free by 2030.

Macquarie sold its entire stake in Safeway Concessions, which owns nine toll road assets across Andhra Pradesh and Gujarat, to VINCI Highways for ₹15,000 crore.
Infrastructure.top_article_title: IRFC sanctions ₹1,000 crore loan to Maharashtra power utility in first FY27 deal

Labour.summary: The All India Bank Employees' Association requested Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman to order a CBI probe into Atanu Chakraborty’s resignation from HDFC Bank over ethical concerns. The salary restructuring under the New Wage Code from April 1 may reduce in-hand pay temporarily as part of a long-term adjustment. SEBI plans a joint initiative to upskill independent directors and strengthen corporate governance.
Labour.top_article_title: Bank staff association urges FM for CBI probe into Atanu Chakraborty’s resignation from HDFC Bank

Health.summary:
Why India’s diabetes epidemic is a policy failure: Stagnating wages, declining food quality, and a national focus on low-cost carbohydrates have made basic nutrition unaffordable.

Why vaccines may not be enough to fight spread of dengue fever: Evidence shows dengue vaccines must generate a balanced immune response against all four viral serotypes or they may increase the risk of severe disease in some individuals.

Bengal elections 2026: At Panihati, the mother of the RG Kar Medical College rape and murder victim, who joined the BJP, will contest the West Bengal Assembly elections, citing her fight for justice as her motivation.
Health.top_article_title: Why India’s diabetes epidemic is a policy failure

Fintech.summary:
CRED upgraded its wealth management platform Kuvera to help affluent Indians make prudent investment decisions.

Starting April 10, toll plazas will no longer accept cash, and UPI payments at these locations will cost 1.25 times the usual fee.

Slice is seeking $50–100 million in funding at a lower valuation while also promoting its digital bank offerings amid heightened regulatory scrutiny in the fintech sector.
Fintech.top_article_title: CRED upgrades its wealth management platform Kuvera

Data Privacy.summary: The Indian government confirmed that AI-enabled CCTV cameras will fall under the Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Act’s rules despite no explicit consent mechanism for CCTV-based data collection. The U.S. Trade Representative highlighted concerns regarding provisions in the DPDP Act and IT Rules that could negatively affect businesses operating in India.
Data Privacy.top_article_title: Govt confirms AI-enabled CCTV cameras will be covered under the DPDP rules

DeepTech.summary:
AI’s growing use in India’s courts is raising concerns about potential bias and structural distortions in the legal system.

Xccelera raised Rs 1.2 crore from founders including Ramakant Sharma, Asad Khan, and Amiya Pathak to scale its enterprise automation platform.

Razorpay partnered with OpenAI to enable developers in India to collect payments via Codex and ChatGPT using AI-driven workflows.
DeepTech.top_article_title: Inside India's courts, AI's growing role sparks concern

Agriculture.summary: Global markets face turmoil as war in Iran disrupts oil supplies, causing soaring raw material costs for businesses worldwide, including plastic bag maker Kevin Kelly and India's aluminum plants. BioPrime helps Indian farmers save crops amid weather uncertainty from erratic monsoons and prolonged heatwaves. A UP farmer received ₹14.66 crore tax notices after a firm named Ram Store operated under his name in Delhi’s Naraina Industrial Area, incurring ₹1.02 crore in income tax dues and additional GST liabilities.

articles.title: SC refuses to interfere with NCLAT order allowing Adani to acquire Jaypee group’s assets
articles.summary: The Supreme Court declined to intervene in a National Company Law Appellate Tribunal (NCLAT) order that permits the Adani Group to acquire assets of the Jaypee Group. The bench directed the NCLAT to expedite its decision on a petition filed by Vedanta Limited, which had challenged the acquisition. This ruling impacts corporate governance and insolvency proceedings in India.
articles.raw_summary: The bench asked the appellate tribunal to expeditiously decide on the petition moved by mining group Vedanta Limited.

articles.title: Cash Deposits Not Unexplained Due to Proven Sale Consideration: ITAT Deletes Addition
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that cash deposits during demonetization cannot be treated as unexplained if the source of funds is proven to be from a sale consideration. The tribunal deleted the tax addition under Section 69A, reinforcing that accepted sale proceeds justify the deposits. This decision impacts tax assessments and clarifies the burden of proof for cash transactions during demonetization.
articles.raw_summary: The issue involved addition under Section 69A for demonetization cash deposits. The Tribunal held that once sale consideration was accepted as source, the addition could not be sustained. The post Cash Deposits Not Unexplained Due to Proven Sale Consideration: ITAT Deletes Addition appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: US and Iran agree to provisional ceasefire with Tehran saying it will reopen strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: The US and Iran have agreed to a two-week conditional ceasefire, temporarily reopening the Strait of Hormuz—a key global oil transit route. The deal was brokered by Pakistan after the US abandoned its ultimatum to Iran. The ceasefire includes Iran's 10-point plan, which the US may consider. This development could impact global oil prices and regional stability, with potential indirect consequences for India's energy imports and diplomatic stance.

articles.summary: The Gujarat High Court stayed an FIR filed for non-payment of VAT dues, citing significant delays in filing that weakened the prosecution. The court held that delayed criminal action cannot be mechanically sustained. The ruling highlights the importance of procedural compliance in tax enforcement cases.

articles.title: Contractor Entitled to GST Rate Increase Refund Due to Statutory Price Adjustment Clause
articles.summary: A court ruled that a contractor is entitled to a refund for increased GST rates due to a statutory price adjustment clause in their contract. The court held that denying the differential GST refund was discriminatory and that state actions in the dispute were arbitrary. The ruling underscores the need for compensation when tax rates change during contract execution.

articles.summary: The High Court ruled that a taxpayer cannot claim a deduction under Section 80P if their income tax return is filed late. The court directed the taxpayer to seek condonation of the delay under Section 119(2)(b) of the Income Tax Act. This emphasizes the importance of adhering to statutory deadlines for tax benefits. The ruling highlights strict compliance requirements for claiming deductions.
articles.raw_summary: The court held that delay in filing returns can bar deduction under Section 80P. It directed the taxpayer to seek condonation under Section 119(2)(b). articles.title: Crypto/VDA Taxation: Income-tax Act 1961 vs 2025 Framework
articles.summary: The article compares the existing Income-tax Act, 1961 with the new 2025 tax framework for cryptocurrency and Virtual Digital Assets (VDAs). The key change removes the minimum threshold for Tax Deducted at Source (TDS) on crypto transactions, making even small trades taxable. This introduces stricter compliance requirements with full transaction-level tracking. The analysis highlights the shift toward more rigorous oversight of crypto transactions in India.
articles.raw_summary: The new law removes the minimum threshold for TDS on crypto transactions, making even small trades taxable. The key takeaway is stricter compliance with full transaction-level tracking. The post Crypto/VDA Taxation: Income-tax Act 1961 vs 2025 Framework appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Jan Vishwas (Amendment of Provisions) Act, 2026 – Decriminalisation of 79 Laws & 784 Provisions
articles.summary: The Jan Vishwas (Amendment of Provisions) Act, 2026, passed by the Indian government, decriminalizes minor regulatory violations by replacing criminal penalties with civil fines and adjudication processes across 79 laws and 784 provisions. This reform aims to reduce the burden on courts and simplify compliance for businesses and individuals. The law was enacted to streamline regulatory enforcement while maintaining accountability through civil penalties.
articles.raw_summary: The Act removes criminal penalties for minor regulatory defaults across 79 laws. It replaces them with civil penalties and adjudication mechanisms. articles.title: Section 54 Exemption Allowed Without Registration Due to Possession & Payment
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal ruled that Section 54 tax exemption cannot be denied solely because a property purchase agreement was unregistered. The tribunal emphasized that substantial payment and possession of the property are sufficient grounds for claiming the exemption. This decision impacts taxpayers seeking relief under Section 54 of the Income Tax Act.

articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that a cheque dishonour complaint under Section 138 of the Negotiable Instruments (NI) Act cannot be quashed at the pre-trial stage once the complainant has met the required legal conditions. articles.raw_summary: “Once the ingredients of S 138 of the N.I. Act are satisfied by the complainant, the rebuttal of statutory presumption can only be made during trial.”

articles.title: Global Market: Emerging markets lean on global funds, but face rising risk of sudden capital flight
articles.summary: Emerging economies, including India, are increasingly dependent on global investors like hedge funds for funding, with portfolio investments now making up 80% of their capital. This shift, driven by reduced traditional bank involvement, offers cheaper long-term funding but heightens risks of sudden capital outflows. Economies with less developed financial markets face greater vulnerability to these volatile flows, which could destabilize their economies.
articles.raw_summary: Emerging economies now depend heavily on global investors like hedge funds for funding. This shift, driven by a retreat of traditional banks, has doubled portfolio investment share to 80%. While this provided cheaper, longer-term funding, it also increased vulnerability to sudden capital outflows. Economies with shallow markets face greater risks from these volatile flows.

The revised schedule maintains the original exam timings while adjusting all Group I and II dates accordingly. The decision was announced by ICAI, the regulatory body overseeing chartered accountancy education in India.
articles.raw_summary: The ICAI postponed the CA Intermediate exams from 3rd May to 5th May 2026 citing unavoidable reasons. The revised schedule retains exam timings while shifting all Group I and II dates accordingly. The post CA Intermediate May 2026 Exams Rescheduled Due to Unavoidable Reasons appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: GAIL inks long-term LNG carrier pact with Alpha Gas
articles.summary: GAIL India has signed a long-term agreement with Alpha Gas (via its Singapore affiliate Pantheon Maritime) to charter the LNG carrier 'Energy Fidelity'. The pact supports GAIL's LNG import operations, aligning with India's push for energy diversification and cleaner fuel sources. LNG imports are critical for India's energy security amid rising domestic demand and policy focus on reducing fossil fuel dependence.
articles.raw_summary: The agreement has been executed with Pantheon Maritime Services Pvt Ltd, a Singapore-based affiliate of Alpha Gas, for the LNG carrier “Energy Fidelity”.

articles.title: Homebuyers Cannot Claim ITC Benefit in Fully Post-GST Projects: GSTAT
articles.summary: The Goods and Services Tax Appellate Tribunal (GSTAT) ruled that homebuyers cannot claim Input Tax Credit (ITC) benefits in projects fully governed under the GST regime. The tribunal held that since construction, agreements, and payments occurred entirely post-GST, prices already included ITC, and no additional benefit needed to be passed on. This decision clarifies the tax treatment of real estate projects under the GST framework.
articles.raw_summary: GSTAT held that no anti-profiteering violation arises where construction, agreement, and payments occur entirely in the GST regime. Since prices already factored ITC, no benefit was required to be passed on. The post Homebuyers Cannot Claim ITC Benefit in Fully Post-GST Projects: GSTAT appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Nvidia acquisition of SchedMD sparks worry among AI specialists about software access
articles.summary: Nvidia announced its acquisition of SchedMD, the company behind Slurm, an open-source software used to schedule computing tasks. Slurm is essential for training large language models like those powering AI chatbots and is also used on government supercomputers for tasks such as weather forecasting and nuclear research. The deal has raised concerns among AI specialists about potential restrictions on software access, which could impact AI development and critical infrastructure.

The court clarified that for an offence under Section 304 Part II IPC, it must be proven that the accused committed culpable homicide as defined in Section 299 IPC. This ruling impacts how obscenity and free speech laws are applied in India.
articles.raw_summary: To convict an accused for commission of an offence punishable under Section 304 Part II IPC, it must be proved that the accused has committed culpable homicide as defined in Section 299 IPC. articles.title: Tax on Winnings From Online Games: Section 115BBJ of Income-tax Act, 1961
articles.summary: The Income-tax Act, 1961, under Section 115BBJ, imposes a uniform 30% tax on income from online gaming winnings, regardless of whether the game involves skill or chance. This policy applies to all gaming activities and was introduced by the government as part of tax regulations. The tax is mandatory and does not differentiate based on the nature of the game.
articles.raw_summary: The issue concerns taxation of income from online games. The law mandates a flat 30% tax regardless of skill or chance. The key takeaway is uniform taxation across all gaming activities. The post Tax on Winnings From Online Games: Section 115BBJ of Income-tax Act, 1961 appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.summary: The US has tightened export controls on semiconductor manufacturing equipment to curb China's technological advancement. A committee stated that semiconductors are central to global tech competition and introduced the MATCH Act to strengthen these controls and coordinate a global approach. The move aims to restrict China's access to advanced chipmaking tools, which could impact global supply chains and India's tech industry.

articles.summary: Google is set to launch a $15 billion data centre project in Visakhapatnam on April 28. The project, expected to be Asia's largest, will expand Google's global data centre presence to 12 countries. The initiative highlights India's growing role in the global digital infrastructure landscape and could influence policies related to data storage, digital economy, and regional development.
articles.raw_summary: So far, Google and its subsidiaries have data centres in 11 countries - the United States, Taiwan, Japan, Singapore, Ireland, the Netherlands, Denmark, Finland, Germany, Belgium, and Chile, spread across 29 geographic locations. The data centre cluster in Visakhapatnam is expected to be Asia's largest.

articles.title: What If Your Weight Could Make Your Holiday Illegal?
articles.summary: The article highlights regulations in Santorini and Shimla that prohibit tourists over 100 kg from riding donkeys and limit animal loads to one-fifth of their body weight. These rules aim to protect animals from overwork but are specific to foreign tourist destinations and do not involve Indian policy or governance.
articles.raw_summary: From Santorini to Shimla, the question is no longer just about holiday and travel, but about the invisible weight animals are forced to carry. On the steep stone paths of Santorini, something quietly radical has entered the tourism economy: a weighing scale. Since 2018, tourists over 100 kg (200 pounds) have not been allowed to ride donkeys, and no animal may carry more than one-fifth of its body weight. Oceans absorb most of the planet's excess heat, but the consequences of rising SST—such as marine ecosystem disruptions and extreme weather—are often overshadowed by discussions on air temperatures. This trend underscores the urgent need for climate action, including policy measures to address ocean warming and its impacts.

articles.summary: A Tamil Nadu court sentenced nine policemen to death for their role in the custodial torture and deaths of a father and son, a case that shocked the nation six years ago. The ruling addresses severe violations of human rights and police accountability. This decision underscores the judiciary's role in upholding civil liberties and deterring custodial abuse in India.

articles.title: Construction, award of highway projects at 7-year low in 2025-26
articles.summary: Highway construction and new project awards in India during 2025-26 dropped to a seven-year low, with less than 10,000 km built and only 7,000 km awarded. The decline is linked to stricter land availability conditions and fewer state highways being converted to National Highways. Government agencies are now prioritizing economic corridors and expressways to avoid delays and cost overruns.

This decline is attributed to stricter land availability pre-conditions and fewer state highways being converted to National Highways. Agencies are prioritizing economic corridors and expressways to avoid project delays and cost escalations.

articles.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump has threatened to launch strikes on Iranian power plants and bridges if Iran continues to block the Strait of Hormuz, a vital oil shipping route. The program is being implemented in collaboration with local authorities to support tribal farmers.

articles.title: Russia, China veto UN resolution on reopening Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Russia and China vetoed a UN resolution proposed by Bahrain and supported by the U.S. to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. The draft resolution received 11 votes in favor, 2 against, and 2 abstentions. The Strait of Hormuz is a key global oil transit route, and its closure could disrupt energy supplies, impacting countries like India that rely on oil imports.

articles.title: Centre asks states to double daily supply of 5-kg LPG cylinders for migrant workers
articles.summary: States and Union Territories are directed to double the daily supply of 5-kg LPG cylinders for migrant workers, ensuring targeted distribution with Oil Marketing Company support. The Centre assures a stable overall LPG supply, dismissing reports of shortages at petrol pumps and LPG distributors. Over 6.75 lakh 5-kg cylinders have been sold since March 23.

The Centre assures a stable overall LPG supply, dismissing reports of shortages at petrol pumps and LPG distributors. Over 6.75 lakh 5-kg cylinders have been sold since March 23.

articles.title: UP plans relief for 22,000 madrasa modernisation teachers after 26-month job loss
articles.summary: The Uttar Pradesh government plans to provide relief to 22,000 madrasa teachers who lost their jobs after a modernization program was terminated in 2023–2024. Minister of State for Minority Welfare Danish Azad Ansari stated that the teachers had been unemployed for 26 months due to the program's end. The state government is now addressing the issue, indicating a policy response to the employment crisis faced by these educators.
articles.raw_summary: Minister of State for Minority Welfare Danish Azad Ansari claimed that thousands of teachers were unemployed due to the program's termination in 2023–2024.

articles.title: Raghav Chadha vs AAP: ‘Jo darr gaya, samjho marr gaya,’ Saurabh Bharadwaj responds to MP's video message, 'soft PR'
articles.summary: It is better to take up more serious issues than samosas in Parliament, AAP leader Saurabh Bharadwaj said in a message addressing Raghav Chadha.
articles.raw_summary: It is better to take up more serious issues than samosas in Parliament, AAP leader Saurabh Bharadwaj said in a message addressing Raghav Chadha.

articles.title: MIB extends TRP freeze on TV news channels by four weeks
articles.summary: The Ministry of Information and Broadcasting (MIB) has extended the freeze on Television Rating Points (TRP) for TV news channels by four weeks. The order cites the ongoing West Asia conflict as the reason for the extension. This decision impacts news broadcasters who rely on TRP metrics for advertising revenue and regulatory compliance.
articles.raw_summary: The MIB order also states that the West Asia conflict situation “still largely prevails,” which is why the suspension has been extended

articles.title: Moody’s cuts India growth to 6% as West Asia conflict hits oil and LPG imports
articles.summary: Moody’s has cut India’s FY27 GDP growth forecast to 6% citing the West Asia conflict, rising oil and LPG prices, and inflation risks, warning that higher energy costs may slow consumption and investment.
articles.raw_summary: Moody’s has cut India’s FY27 GDP growth forecast to 6% citing the West Asia conflict, rising oil and LPG prices, and inflation risks, warning that higher energy costs may slow consumption and investment.

articles.title: Plastics group seeks 'essential commodity' status amid polymer price shock
articles.summary: The plastics industry body has requested the government to classify plastics as an 'essential commodity' due to a severe cost and supply shock. Raw material prices have surged by 50-70%, while availability is erratic, logistics costs are higher, and LPG shortages affect processing units. The industry seeks policy relief to stabilize prices and ensure supply continuity.
articles.raw_summary: The industry body said the sector is facing a severe cost and supply shock, with raw material prices rising by 50-70%, along with erratic availability, extended lead times, higher logistics costs and shortage of LPG used by several processing units.

articles.title: India’s data centre boom is hitting an invisible ceiling
articles.summary: India rapidly expands data centres on low costs and AI demand, but faces power transmission, connectivity and sustainability constraints that may limit long term growth
articles.raw_summary: The challenge isn't capital or intent, but the lack of infrastructure that can support the data centres. However, there are companies that have the potential to fill these gaps.

articles.summary: Anthropic will stop supporting third-party AI tools like OpenClaw for its Claude subscription. Users will need to pay separately for OpenClaw usage starting soon. The change is part of Anthropic's commercial policy and does not involve Indian government actions or regulations.

articles.title: DGCA eases pilot duty norms for long-haul flights to ensure smooth operations
articles.summary: The Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA) has relaxed pilot duty norms for long-haul flights to ensure smoother airline operations. The decision aims to address operational challenges while maintaining safety standards. The DGCA, India's aviation regulator, issued this directive to streamline flight schedules and crew management.
articles.raw_summary: DGCA eases pilot duty norms for long-haul flights to ensure smooth operations

articles.title: US Allies Work On Plan B For Hormuz Strait If Trump Walks Away
articles.summary: More than 40 US allies, including key partners, met to discuss contingency plans for reopening the Strait of Hormuz. The move signals deep concern over the waterway's crisis, triggered by US-Iran tensions under President Trump's policies. The Strait is a critical global oil transit route, and any disruption could impact energy supplies. India, heavily reliant on oil imports, may face indirect consequences from such geopolitical developments.
articles.raw_summary: More than 40 US allies met Thursday to discuss plans to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, in a signal to President Donald Trump of the deep concern across the international community about the crisis in the waterway triggered by his war on Iran.

articles.title: LS speaker & RS chairman reject notice for motion to remove CEC Gyanesh Kumar
articles.summary: The Lok Sabha Speaker and Rajya Sabha Chairman rejected a notice for a motion to remove Chief Election Commissioner (CEC) Gyanesh Kumar. This involves parliamentary procedure and governance, as the CEC's removal requires approval from both houses. The decision impacts the functioning of the Election Commission of India, which oversees elections in the country.
articles.raw_summary: LS speaker   RS chairman reject notice for motion to remove CEC Gyanesh Kumar

articles.title: WeWork India to set up 5 co-working centres in South India
articles.summary: WeWork India is expanding its flexible workspace offerings. The company has leased over 7 lakh square feet of office space in Bengaluru, Hyderabad, and Chennai. Five new centres will be established, adding approximately 12,000 desks. These new locations will cater to enterprises and growing companies. WeWork India now operates in eight cities across India.

articles.title: Kharg Island under attack: Iran's 'crown jewel' targeted in fresh US strikes
articles.summary: Iran's Kharg Island, a key oil export hub, was targeted in US military strikes ahead of a deadline related to the Strait of Hormuz. The incident involves the US and Iran, with potential consequences for global oil markets. India, as a major oil importer, may face energy supply or price volatility due to the escalation.

articles.title: Once the weathervane of Tamil Nadu politics, how Ramadoss lost his grip on PMK & its core Vanniyar base
articles.summary: Ramadoss, a key Tamil Nadu politician, has allied with V.K. Sasikala ahead of the 23 April polls. The alliance aims to unite the Vanniyar and Thevar communities, which both leaders represent. This political shift could impact future governance and policy decisions in the state. The move is seen as a strategic move to consolidate community support.
articles.raw_summary: Ahead of the 23 April polls, Ramadoss has sided with V.K. Sasikala. The alliance is seen as a ploy to enmesh Vanniyars and Thevars together, the communities both leaders represent.

articles.title: Oil production to take hit, output from Middle East expected to drop by 9 million barrel per day
articles.summary: The U.S. Energy Information Administration estimates that oil production from the Middle East will decline by around 9 million barrels per day in April 2026. This reduction could impact global oil supply and prices, with potential consequences for India's energy imports, fuel costs, and economic stability. The forecast is based on agency data and does not involve direct Indian government action.
articles.raw_summary: Oil production from the Middle East is expected to reduce by around 9 million barrels per day in April 2026, according to United States government agency Energy Information Administration's estimates.

articles.title: US Energy agency says fuel prices may keep rising for months after Strait of Hormuz reopens: All we know
articles.summary: The US Energy Information Administration warns that fuel prices may continue rising for months even after the Strait of Hormuz reopens. Petrol prices in the US have already peaked at $4.30 per gallon. The situation could impact global oil supply chains, including India's energy imports and inflation rates.
articles.raw_summary: Fuel prices are expected to rise for months even after the Strait of Hormuz reopens, according to the US Energy Information Administration. Petrol prices have peaked at $4.30 a gallon in April.

articles.title: Prices of rice, wheat, atta and pulses fall despite West Asia war
articles.summary: Despite the West Asia war causing potential supply disruptions, the prices of essential food items like rice, wheat, atta, and pulses in India have fallen. Consumer Affairs Secretary Nidhi Khare attributed this stability to timely government interventions, including buffer stock releases and supply-side measures. The article underscores how policy actions prevented sharp price spikes in critical food commodities.
articles.raw_summary: Timely interventions, including buffer stock releases and supply-side measures, prevented any sharp spikes despite external shocks, says consumer affairs secretary Nidhi Khare.

articles.title: The British Military Sees Youth Jobs Crisis as an Opportunity
articles.summary: Young people in Britain facing high unemployment are increasingly joining the military. The British government is leveraging this trend to address both its armed forces' recruitment shortfall and youth joblessness. The military sees this as an opportunity to strengthen its ranks while providing employment to desperate job seekers.
articles.raw_summary: Young Britons in desperate search of a job are increasingly signing up to the military, with the government looking to exploit a trend that could help address both its youth unemployment crisis and depleted armed forces.

articles.title: Meloni Visits Gulf to Bolster Energy Ties to War-Hit States
articles.summary: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni made a surprise two-day visit to Saudi Arabia to meet leaders, including Crown Prince Mohammed Bin Salman, aiming to bolster energy ties with war-affected Gulf states. The visit is part of efforts to enhance Italy's domestic energy security amid global supply disruptions. The move could influence India's energy import strategies and foreign policy considerations in the region.
articles.raw_summary: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni flew to Saudi Arabia on a surprise two-day visit to the Gulf, with plans to meet leaders including Crown Prince Mohammed Bin Salman as she aims to strengthen domestic energy security.

articles.title: What’s your money personality? It may decide your market returns
articles.summary: The article explores how an investor's 'money personality'—their emotional response to market volatility—can influence financial decisions and long-term returns. Financial advisors are using behavioral insights to help investors manage fear and risk during market downturns. The piece highlights the role of psychology in financial planning, emphasizing that losses are often locked in by behavior rather than market volatility itself.
articles.raw_summary: When markets fall, behaviour—not volatility—locks in losses. Financial advisors are decoding ‘money personalities’ to help investors manage fear, risk and long-term returns.

articles.summary: Anthropic, an AI company, has paused the release of its most advanced AI model, Claude Mythos, due to concerns that it could discover software vulnerabilities. The model's capabilities raised public safety and national security risks, prompting the company to halt its deployment and launch a new initiative to address cyber threats. The decision highlights growing scrutiny over AI's dual-use potential and the need for safeguards in emerging technologies.
articles.raw_summary: Anthropic's AI model Claude Mythos release has been put on hold by the company. The AI model is capable of discovering software vulnerabilities which have raised concerns for public safety and national security.

The update is part of broader AI tool enhancements but does not involve government policy or regulatory changes in India.

Industries had reportedly set up community kitchens to retain workers amid the scarcity. The move aims to stabilize workforce retention and address energy access for migrant workers.

HT had reported on Monday that some industries were even setting up community kitchens to retain workers.

articles.title: China targets Taiwan's chip prowess to evade global 'containment', Taipei government says
articles.summary: Taiwan's government reports that China is attempting to lure its high-tech industries, including semiconductors and AI, to establish operations in China. This involves illegal poaching of talent and strict Taiwanese laws to prevent advanced technology transfer. The National Security Bureau of Taiwan shared this assessment with lawmakers. The move highlights China's efforts to bypass global containment and strengthen its own tech capabilities.
articles.raw_summary: Taiwan, which China claims as its own territory, frequently reports busting networks ‌of Chinese firms ⁠illegally trying ⁠to poach semiconductor and high-tech talent, and has strict laws to prevent the most advanced technologies from going to China. In ​a report to lawmakers, Taiwan's National Security Bureau said that China is attempting to "lure" Taiwan's high-tech industries, including ​AI and semiconductors, to establish or retain operations in China.

Farmers have been advised to use protective measures like hail nets, harvest crops promptly, and safeguard livestock and produce.

articles.title: Lack of proof, high constitutional bar: Why CEC Gyanesh Kumar’s removal bid was rejected
articles.summary: The Supreme Court rejected a motion to remove Chief Election Commissioner Gyanesh Kumar due to lack of credible evidence and a high constitutional threshold. The court ruled that a prima facie case must be established for such a motion to be admitted. The decision highlights the legal and procedural barriers for removing constitutional authorities in India.
articles.raw_summary: It added that for such a motion to be admitted, there would need to be “credible material disclosing a prima facie case”.

articles.title: No exit polls from 7 am of April 9 till 6.30 pm of April 29: EC ahead of elections
articles.summary: The Election Commission (EC) of India has prohibited the conduct or broadcast of exit polls from 7 am on April 9 until 6:30 pm on April 29, citing violations of Section 126A of the Representation of the People Act, 1951. This restriction applies during the election period to maintain fairness. The EC's directive is a regulatory enforcement action under existing electoral laws.
articles.raw_summary: The EC cautioned that conducting or broadcasting exit polls in the period is violative of section 126A of the Representation of the People Act, 1951.

articles.summary: Women's credit uptake has surged, now representing Rs 76 lakh crore or 26% of total system credit in 2025, a near five-fold increase since 2017. Their engagement with formal credit and asset ownership is rapidly expanding.

articles.title: Middle East turmoil puts India’s trade, energy flows at risk: Report

Higher energy prices and potential slowdowns in Gulf economies could lead to sustained inflation and slower growth for India.

articles.title: ‘Draft’ tag in Pakistan PM Shehbaz Sharif’s X post edit history raises questions
articles.summary: Pakistan's Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif edited a post on X (formerly Twitter) before former U.S. President Donald Trump announced a two-week ceasefire. The edit history of the post has raised questions about its timing and intent. The incident involves diplomatic communication between Pakistan and the U.S., which may have indirect implications for India's foreign policy or regional stability.
articles.raw_summary: The post by Pakistan PM Shehbaz Sharif, shared before Donald Trump announced a two-week ceasefire, has gone viral.

articles.title: Afghanistan says talks with Pakistan to end fighting were 'constructive'
articles.summary: Afghanistan and Pakistan held talks in China's Urumqi to address border fighting. The discussions were described as constructive by Afghanistan. The outcome of these negotiations may impact regional security dynamics, which is relevant for India's foreign policy and defence considerations.
articles.raw_summary: The talks began last week in the western Chinese city of Urumqi following an invitation by China.

articles.title: Pakistan done playing peacemaker? May end up on US-Israel side vs Iran due to Saudi defence pact
articles.summary: Pakistan and Saudi Arabia signed a Strategic Mutual Defence Agreement last year, where both nations agreed to treat an attack on one as an attack on both. This pact may influence Pakistan's alignment in regional conflicts, potentially shifting its stance toward the US-Israel side against Iran. The agreement could have broader implications for South Asian geopolitics and India's strategic interests.
articles.raw_summary: Pakistan and Saudi Arabia signed Strategic Mutual Defence Agreement last year, pledging that any attack on either nation would be treated as one on both.

articles.title: ‘Tomorrow Inshallah’: Iran after Trump's 'civilization will die' threat
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump warned that 'a whole civilization will die tonight' unless Iran reopens the Strait of Hormuz, a key shipping route for global oil supplies. The threat follows heightened tensions between the US and Iran, raising concerns about potential disruptions to oil shipments that India heavily relies on. The situation could impact India's energy imports and regional stability, though no direct policy action is mentioned.
articles.raw_summary: US President Donald Trump on Tuesday warned that “a whole civilization will die tonight” unless Tehran opens the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Hormuz 'deadline' just hours away, here are 3 ways the Strait could be opened

The conflict could disrupt energy supplies, impacting countries like India that rely on oil imports. articles.title: Taliban-shaped albatross around Pakistan’s neck
articles.summary: The article examines Pakistan's long-standing strategy of viewing Afghanistan as part of its 'strategic depth,' which has shaped its foreign policy. It argues that this approach has become a burden for Pakistan, particularly in the context of Taliban rule in Afghanistan. The analysis suggests that this dynamic has broader implications for regional stability, including for India, given the shared border and security concerns.
articles.raw_summary: Pakistan has always considered Afghanistan as a key part of its strategic depth

articles.title: Census 2027 self-enumeration explained: Steps to complete it easily
articles.summary: The Census of India 2027 will introduce a self-enumeration option, allowing individuals to complete their census forms online via the official portal without waiting for an enumerator. This initiative aims to streamline data collection and improve efficiency in the decennial census process. The move is part of the government's digital governance efforts to modernize administrative procedures.
articles.raw_summary: With Census 2027, you don’t have to wait for someone to come home; you can just fill it out yourself online by visiting the official census self-enumeration portal.

articles.title: Explainer-Can Iran charge fees for ships to transit the Strait of Hormuz?
articles.summary: Iran is considering imposing fees on ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz, a key shipping route for global oil trade, as part of its proposals to end the conflict with Israel and the United States. The move follows weeks of disrupted traffic through the waterway, which could affect energy supplies and shipping costs worldwide. The Strait of Hormuz is a vital route for India's oil imports from the Middle East.
articles.raw_summary: Iran wants to charge fees for ships to pass through the Strait of Hormuz as part of its proposals to end the war with Israel and the United States after blocking most traffic through the crucial energy waterway for weeks.
